questions.question: Sodium chloride is a liquid at 900 °C. How are the particles arranged and how do the particles move in sodium chloride at 900 °C? arrangement of particles motion of particles A regular vibrate about a fixed point, B regular                               move                               randomly, C random vibrate about a fixed point, D random                               move                               randomly
questions.text: Sodium chloride is a liquid at 900 °C. How are the particles arranged and how do the particles move in sodium chloride at 900 °C? arrangement of particles motion of particles A regular vibrate about a fixed point, B regular                               move                               randomly, C random vibrate about a fixed point, D random                               move                               randomly
options: A regular vibrate about a fixed point
B regular                               move                               randomly
C random vibrate about a fixed point
D random                               move                               randomly

questions.question: Which statement about an atom of fluorine, F 19 9 , is correct? A It contains more protons than neutrons., B It contains a total of 28 protons, neutrons and electrons., C Its isotopes contain different numbers of protons., D Its nucleus contains 9 neutrons.

B It contains a total of 28 protons, neutrons and electrons.

questions.question: Calcium reacts with chlorine to produce calcium chloride. What happens when a calcium ion forms during this reaction? A The calcium atom gains one electron., B The calcium atom gains two electrons., C The calcium atom loses one electron., D The calcium atom loses two electrons.
questions.text: Calcium reacts with chlorine to produce calcium chloride. What happens when a calcium ion forms during this reaction? A The calcium atom gains one electron., B The calcium atom gains two electrons., C The calcium atom loses one electron., D The calcium atom loses two electrons.
options: A The calcium atom gains one electron.
B The calcium atom gains two electrons.
C The calcium atom loses one electron.
D The calcium atom loses two electrons.

questions.question: Which row describes the formation of single covalent bonds in methane? A atoms share a pair of electrons both atoms gain a noble gas electronic structure B atoms share a pair of electrons both atoms have the same number of electrons in their outer shell C electrons are transferred from one atom to another both atoms gain a noble gas electronic structure D electrons are transferred from one atom to another both atoms have the same number of electrons in their outer shell 4
questions.text: Which row describes the formation of single covalent bonds in methane? A atoms share a pair of electrons both atoms gain a noble gas electronic structure B atoms share a pair of electrons both atoms have the same number of electrons in their outer shell C electrons are transferred from one atom to another both atoms gain a noble gas electronic structure D electrons are transferred from one atom to another both atoms have the same number of electrons in their outer shell 4

questions.question: Diamond and graphite have giant covalent structures of carbon atoms. Which statement describes graphite? A It has a strong, rigid three-dimensional structure., B It has four strong covalent bonds between each carbon atom., C It has layers, which can slide over each other., D It has no free electrons, so does not conduct electricity.
questions.text: Diamond and graphite have giant covalent structures of carbon atoms. Which statement describes graphite? A It has a strong, rigid three-dimensional structure., B It has four strong covalent bonds between each carbon atom., C It has layers, which can slide over each other., D It has no free electrons, so does not conduct electricity.
options: A It has a strong, rigid three-dimensional structure.
B It has four strong covalent bonds between each carbon atom.
C It has layers, which can slide over each other.
D It has no free electrons, so does not conduct electricity.

questions.question: Hydrogen  peroxide  solution  decomposes  very  slowly  at  room  temperature  to  produce  oxygen gas. This gas forms a rising foam when liquid detergent is added. Five test-tubes are half filled with hydrogen peroxide solution. A drop of liquid detergent is added to each one. Different metal oxides are added to four of the test-tubes and the height of the foam formed after 1 minute is measured. The results are shown. metal oxide height of foam / cm no metal oxide added 0.1 aluminium oxide 0.1 calcium oxide 0.2 copper(II) oxide 2.3 manganese(IV) oxide 5.4 Which conclusion can be drawn from these results? A Metal oxides do not affect the rate of this reaction., B All metal oxides increase the rate of this reaction and act as catalysts., C Manganese( IV) oxide is the best catalyst of the four metal oxides tested., D Only transition element oxides increase the rate of this reaction.
questions.text: Hydrogen  peroxide  solution  decomposes  very  slowly  at  room  temperature  to  produce  oxygen gas. This gas forms a rising foam when liquid detergent is added. Five test-tubes are half filled with hydrogen peroxide solution. A drop of liquid detergent is added to each one. Different metal oxides are added to four of the test-tubes and the height of the foam formed after 1 minute is measured. The results are shown. metal oxide height of foam / cm no metal oxide added 0.1 aluminium oxide 0.1 calcium oxide 0.2 copper(II) oxide 2.3 manganese(IV) oxide 5.4 Which conclusion can be drawn from these results? A Metal oxides do not affect the rate of this reaction., B All metal oxides increase the rate of this reaction and act as catalysts., C Manganese( IV) oxide is the best catalyst of the four metal oxides tested., D Only transition element oxides increase the rate of this reaction.
options: A Metal oxides do not affect the rate of this reaction.
B All metal oxides increase the rate of this reaction and act as catalysts.
C Manganese( IV) oxide is the best catalyst of the four metal oxides tested.
D Only transition element oxides increase the rate of this reaction.

questions.question: When  blue-green  crystals  of  nickel( II)  sulfate  are  heated,  water  is  produced  and  a  yellow  solid remains. When water is added to the yellow solid, the blue-green colour returns. Which process describes these changes? A combustion, B corrosion, C neutralisation, D reversible reaction 6
questions.text: When  blue-green  crystals  of  nickel( II)  sulfate  are  heated,  water  is  produced  and  a  yellow  solid remains. options: A combustion
B corrosion
C neutralisation
D reversible reaction 6

questions.question: In a blast furnace, iron is extracted when iron(III) oxide reacts with carbon monoxide. The equation is shown. Fe 2 O 3 +  3CO  →  2Fe  +  3CO 2 Which substance is oxidised and which is reduced? oxidised         reduced A CO               Fe 2 O 3, B CO 2 Fe, C Fe                 CO 2, D Fe 2 O 3 CO
questions.text: In a blast furnace, iron is extracted when iron(III) oxide reacts with carbon monoxide. The equation is shown. Fe 2 O 3 +  3CO  →  2Fe  +  3CO 2 Which substance is oxidised and which is reduced? oxidised         reduced A CO               Fe 2 O 3, B CO 2 Fe, C Fe                 CO 2, D Fe 2 O 3 CO

B CO 2 Fe
C Fe                 CO 2
D Fe 2 O 3 CO

questions.question: Four  different  solutions  are  separately  tested  with  blue  litmus  and  with  methyl  orange.  Each solution is known to be either acidic or alkaline. The results are shown. solution result with blue litmus result with methyl orange 1                   red                       red 2                   red                    yellow 3                   blue                    yellow 4                   blue                    yellow Which statement is correct? A Solutions 1 and 4 are acidic., B Solutions 1 and 2 are alkaline., C Solutions 3 and 4 are alkaline., D Solutions 3 and 4 are acidic.

questions.question: Iron is extracted from its ore in a blast furnace. Hematite, coke, limestone and hot air are added to the furnace. Which explanation is not correct? A Coke burns and produces a high temperature., B Hematite is the ore containing the iron as iron( III) oxide., C Hot air provides the oxygen for the burning., D Limestone reduces the iron( III) oxide to iron.

questions.question: Why is ethanol a member of the homologous series of alcohols but propane is not? A Ethanol has two carbon atoms per molecule but propane has three., B Ethanol can be made from ethene but propane is obtained from petroleum., C Ethanol is a liquid but propane is a gas., D Ethanol contains the same functional group as other alcohols but propane does not.
questions.text: Why is ethanol a member of the homologous series of alcohols but propane is not? A Ethanol has two carbon atoms per molecule but propane has three., B Ethanol can be made from ethene but propane is obtained from petroleum., C Ethanol is a liquid but propane is a gas., D Ethanol contains the same functional group as other alcohols but propane does not.
options: A Ethanol has two carbon atoms per molecule but propane has three.
B Ethanol can be made from ethene but propane is obtained from petroleum.
C Ethanol is a liquid but propane is a gas.
D Ethanol contains the same functional group as other alcohols but propane does not.
